In his richly reported new book,  Hated by All the Right People: Tucker Carlson and the Unraveling of the Conservative Mind , Jason Zengerle tracks the evolution of the mainstream conservative journalist for  The   Weekly Standard , CNN and FOX News into a prominent figure in the far-right media ecosystem whose commentary increasingly descends into open antisemitism. 
 Zengerle, a veteran political reporter, ruminates over Carlson’s troubling transition from magazine writer to cable news pundit to his current position as a widely followed podcast host whose credulous interviews with Nazi sympathizers and Holocaust deniers, among others, have done little to dampen his influence in the MAGA movement he helped build. 
 In a recent interview with  Jewish Insider , Zengerle, whose book will be published Tuesday, warned that Carlson’s efforts to smuggle antisemitic views into mainstream discourse should not be taken lightly. 
 “Tucker has credibility, and he comes across as a credible person,” Zengerle said. “That he’s giving voice to these really pretty fringe and dangerous sentiments is not to be underestimated, because people trust him.” 
 Whether Carlson personally believes the “awful things” he promotes, Zengerle writes in his book, “matters less than that he says them at all, and that millions of people — members of Congress, titans of industry, the president and just everyday Americans — listen to and take their cues from him.” 
 “What matters is that by saying these things, Carlson has finally achieved the fame, power and influence that for so long eluded him,” he adds. 
 Zengerle, 52, was recently hired as a staff writer for  The New Yorker , and has previously contributed to  The New York Times Magazine ,  GQ  and  The New Republic , among other publications. His book on Carlson is his first. 
 Speaking with JI last week, Zengerle discussed Carlson’s professional ascent, his motivations for demonizing Israel and why conservative Jews are so frightened by his pot

article_content:
Jan. 25, 2016 
 BLOOMBERG 
 President Obama and House Speaker Ryan may sit down together for a long-anticipated meeting as soon as next week. The two men haven’t spent significant time together since Ryan, a Wisconsin Republican, was sworn in as speaker in October. 
 Last year topped 2014 as the hottest in recorded history, as the El Nino compounded the effects of climate change to wreak havoc on weather patterns around the world, according to the United Nations’ World Meteorological Organization. 
 New York and New Jersey restored transportation services on Sunday and strove to be ready for a near-normal workweek, while Washington struggled to overcome the blizzard that blanketed parts of the Mid-Atlantic region with two feet of snow. Stock, bond, and commodities markets in New York are planning to operate on regular schedules. 
 The odds are “vanishingly small” that recent years of record warmth aren’t due to human emissions of greenhouse gases, researchers in the U.S. and Germany said, adding to pressure on world governments to cut back on fossil fuel use. 
 Morgan Stanley named 156 employees to the title of managing director, five more than last year. Fifty-four percent of those selected for the company’s highest non-executive title work in the investment-banking and trading division. 
 A Thai airline will allow passengers to purchase seats for life-like dolls that their owners treat as real children due to their belief that they possess supernatural powers. 
 The $15 trillion rout in global equity markets since May is reawakening the lure of gold for investors seeking safety. Hedge funds more than doubled their net-long position in bullion last week, just three weeks after they were the most-bearish ever. Investor holdings of gold through exchange-traded products are expanding at the fastest pace in a year, and the value of the ETPs has jumped by $3 billion in 2016.

input_article: As the 2026 T20 World Cup approaches, former India stalwart and World Cup-winner Suresh Raina has thrown his weight behind Sanju Samson, calling for the team management to show the same level of patience with the Kerala wicketkeeper-batter as they once did with captain Suryakumar Yadav. Despite Samson's recent lean patch -- averaging less than 20 since January 2025 -- Raina believes the wicket-keeper batter's "class" and ability to handle both pace and spin make him an indispensable asset for the defending champions.
article_content: Not Samson Or Gill! Raina Picks India's Openers For 2026 T20 World Cup Last Updated: August 30, 2025, 09:26 IST The 2026 edition of the ICC Men's T20 World Cup will be played in the months of February and March across multiple venues in India and Sri Lanka. Suresh Raina picks India's openers for T20 World Cup 2025. (Picture Credit: PTI) The 2026 edition of the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup will be played in India and Sri Lanka in February and March next year. A total of 20 teams will take part in the shortest format mega-event, which was last played in June 2024 in the West Indies and the USA and won by the Rohit Sharma-led Indian men’s cricket team. The Indian team, which tops the ICC rankings as well, will be one of the favourites to lift the title for the record-extending third time next year. Ahead of the shortest format megaevent in the first quarter of 2026, former middle-order batter Suresh Raina, who was the first Indian to score a century in T20Is, was asked to pick his two opening batters for India in the T20 World Cup 2026. Yashasvi Jaiswal, Shubman Gill, Abhishek Sharma, Sanju Samson, Ruturaj Gaikwad and KL Rahul are the ones who are contenders for the role, but according to Raina, Jaiswal should be one, and the other could be one out of Abhishek Sharma and Priyansh Arya. Jaiswal has 723 runs to his credit in 23 T20Is played so far, whereas Abhishek, who also tops the ICC T20I batter’s ranking, has 535 runs to his name in 17 matches. On the other hand, Arya is yet to make his debut for India, but he impressed with his batting in IPL 2025 for Punjab Kings. “I think… mujhe lagta hai Yashasvi ek hai  (Yashasvi is one). They can also look for Priyansh Arya; Abhishek Sharma is there, and Sanju Samson is there. KL Rahul is also there. He has performed wherever he got a chance. Ruturaj Gaikwad has also scored many runs. But I would prefer Abhishek Sharma. Shubman Gill can be captain as well; you never know," Raina said on Shubhankar Mishra’s YouTube channel. “ Mujhe

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia 
 
 
 
 The  2026 ICC Men's T20 World Cup  will be the tenth edition of the  ICC Men's T20 World Cup , a biennial  world cup  for  cricket  in  Twenty20 International  (T20I) format, organized by the  International Cricket Council  (ICC). It will be co-hosted by  Board of Control for Cricket in India  and  Sri Lanka Cricket  from 7 February to 8 March 2026. [ 1 ] [ 2 ]  A total of twenty teams will compete in 55 matches across five venues in India and three in Sri Lanka. [ 3 ]  Each team was allowed to have a squad of 15 players and was required submit the provisional squad to the ICC by 8 January 2026. The teams are allowed to make changes to their squads until 31 January 2026. [ 4 ] 
 Sri Lanka announced their preliminary squad on 19 December 2025. [ 5 ]  On 20 December 2025, India was the first team to announce its squad for the tournament. [ 6 ]  On 30 December 2025, England was the second team to announce its squad for the tournament.
 Players are listed in shirt number order and withdrawn players are placed at the bottom of the table followed by standby players. Given ages are as of 7 February 2026. Withdrawn/dropped players are struck through, and standby players are indicated by a dagger symbol (†). Domestic teams are the recent  T20 league  teams that the players belonged to before the T20 World Cup and are only listed for countries with available data.
 
 
 Squad announcement date : 12 January 2026 [ 13 ] 
 Coach :   Ryan Cook [ 14 ] 
 Squad announcement date : 2 January 2026 [ 32 ] 
 Coach :   Justin Sammons [ 33 ]

article_content:
Poonam Apraj  
 
 MUMBAI  
 
 A 32-year-old law student, also  the founder of ChakaChak  Dadar, Chandrakant Krishna  Kamble, was brutally assaulted  late Sunday night in what he  alleged as a pre-planned  attack. The accused has been  identified as local resident  Deepak Dinesh Chougule.  According  to the com- pla int ,  Ka mbl e  resides with  his mother  Shakuntala,  60, and sister Kavita, 37, at  Senapati Bapat Road, Dadar  (W). Kamble said Chougule  is the nephew of another  resident in the same build- ing. A few months ago,  Chougule allegedly had a dis- pute with their mutual  friends while on a trip. On  returning to the building, the  argument continued.  Kamble, who was merely  standing nearby, was ver- bally abused and threatened  allegedly without provocation.  He had filed an online police  complaint at that time.  On December 7, around  10.30pm, Kamble visited a  friend’s photography studio in  Parel with two acquaintances.  They returned home at around  11.45pm. While Kamble was  heading home, Chougule alleg- edly emerged from building  No. 1 and pushed him towards  a duct area. Kamble managed  to escape, but the assault esca- lated. Chougule allegedly  pushed Kamble onto a motor- cycle and punched him on the  nose, asked his associates to  “bring a rod” and then  threatened “murder”. After  hearing Kamble’s cries, his  mother and friends rushed  and took him to Hinduja  Hospital. Doctors confirmed a  torn lower lip, a broken tooth,  and nasal fracture. Kamble  mentioned that residents pan- icked and shut their doors. He  further alleged that Chaugule  threatened him saying he  would “teach him a lesson” if  he ever approached the police.  Based on the complaint, the  police registered a case for  causing grievous injury and  issuing death threats.  
 
 mumbai
